ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Make cards to give to friends and family on special occasions.
[ ] Keep a series of color swatches of leaves as they change through the Fall.
[ ] Use any medium to draw or paint a picture of a bike. 
[ ] Draw a picture of your favorite pair of shoes without lifting your pen or pencil off the paper. This is called a continuous line drawing. 
[ ] Use any medium to draw everything you see on your desk or table.

[ ] Do a portrait of a friend or family member as a gift.
[ ] Paint the same subject in watercolor and in pastel.
[ ] Do a portrait in charcoal.
[ ] Design a pattern for new wallpaper in your kitchen. 
[ ] Paint a mountainous landscape.
[ ] Use pastels to paint a picture of a tree in unconventional colors; i.e. blue leaves with a purple and green trunk and bright orange flowers.
[ ] Create a design with a black ink pen or Sharpie using only circles and rectangles. Vary the size of each. Make some mass shapes and some outlines.

[ ] Paint a series of the same tree as it changes over the term.
[ ] Paint the same series in pastels and watercolor.
[ ] Observe and paint different bodies of water.
[ ] Create a graphite drawing with shading of an owl in a tree. 
[ ] Create an ideal scene you would like to see out of your window. 
[ ] Use pastels to create a table centerpiece full of 8 to 10 pieces of fruit. 
[ ] Render a zoomed in image of an indoor or outdoor plant from observation. Focus on the texture of the plant.

resources.title: Draw 50 Birds: The Step-by-Step Way to Draw Chickadees, Peacocks, Toucans, Mallards, and Many More of Our Feathered Friends
resources.author: Lee J. Ames and Tony D'Adamo

resources.title: Gardner's Art Through the Ages
resources.author: Fred S. Kleiner, Christin J. Mamiya

resources.title: Portrait Drawing for Kids: A Step-by-Step Guide to Drawing Faces
resources.author: Angela Rizza

resources.title: The Arts: A Visual Encyclopedia
resources.author: DK
resources.rationale: We will be using this as our spine throughout the course. It is a nice overview of global art history. This book was chosen due to its miniscule amount of nudity compared to other art books geared to teens.
